message:
chore(beads): review-pass-7 — 4 HIGH style/docs/security/provenance gaps

Continued repo-hygiene audit focused on style, docs, secrets, and
commit provenance. Workspace baseline steady.

  frankenpandas-thty [HIGH] style: no rustfmt.toml. Swarm agents
                            burn cycles on format churn; each
                            agent's local rustfmt diverges
                            silently because CI's `cargo fmt
                            --check` enforces rustfmt defaults
                            but captures zero explicit style
                            opinions (max_width, imports_granularity,
                            group_imports, edition="2024" for
                            rustfmt itself).

  frankenpandas-ddox [HIGH] docs: `cargo test --doc --workspace`
                            is not in ci.yml. 8 existing doctests
                            across frankenpandas / fp-index /
                            fp-columnar never execute in CI. When
                            br-4clx (release) ships and docs.rs
                            builds the crate, any broken doctest
                            fails the docs build publicly. Tight
                            interlock with br-7cfm (panic contract)
                            — doctests verify the contract in
                            addition to documenting it.

  frankenpandas-hg60 [HIGH] security: no secret scanning. No
                            gitleaks / truffleHog / detect-secrets
                            in CI, no .pre-commit-config.yaml.
                            Only pre-commit hook is the swarm's
                            agent-mail coordination (50-agent-mail.py).
                            Git history is append-only; a leaked
                            credential today is permanently
                            recoverable by any scraper forever.
                            Proposes gitleaks action + pre-commit
                            hook + one-time retroactive history
                            scan.

  frankenpandas-3d5q [HIGH] provenance: no commit-signing policy.
                            git log --show-signature shows no
                            signed commits. Multi-agent swarm has
                            multiple authorial identities
                            (cc-pandas, Dicklesworthstone, others)
                            intermingled; audit of "did a real
                            identity approve this" relies on
                            easily-spoofed Author: fields.
                            crates.io accepts publishes based on
                            API token + Cargo.toml author — no
                            signature check. Proposes SSH signing
                            policy in AGENTS.md + AUTHORS.md with
                            per-agent pubkeys + branch protection
                            requiring signed commits + signed
                            tags for release (br-4clx).

Interlock with prior passes:
- thty (rustfmt) + 1d9y (nightly pin) — pinned nightly stabilizes
  rustfmt version, rustfmt.toml stabilizes opinions. Together
  they remove nearly all style-drift noise.
- ddox (doctest) + 7cfm (panic contract) + 4clx (release) +
  zk1j (API matrix) — four-way interlock. Doctest coverage is
  simultaneously a panic-contract verifier, a release-time
  docs.rs gate, and an input to the coverage matrix.
- hg60 (secret scan) + 36qc (cargo-audit) + 8k1i (SECURITY.md) +
  3d5q (signing) — four halves of supply-chain hygiene: detection
  (audit), prevention (secret scan), private reporting (SECURITY),
  and attestation (signing).
- 3d5q (signing) + 4clx (release workflow) + 6d5s (CONTRIBUTING)
  + iy8u (swarm doctrine) — commit signing is the crypto-backing
  for the swarm-doctrine text iy8u captured.

Running session totals after 7 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL
  25 HIGH
  4 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 32 review beads total.

Each bead has concrete fix shape, blast radius, prerequisite /
interlocking dependencies, and the ratio of CI-minutes cost to
bug-class-prevented value. Zero shipped-code bugs across every
pass. Workspace stays green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-3d5q","title":"[HIGH] provenance: no commit-signing policy or CI signature verification — supply-chain attestation missing before crates.io release","description":"Commit provenance + signing audit.

**Observation.** grep for `gpgsign|signed|ssh` in AGENTS.md returns nothing. grep for `commit.gpgsign` in the repo returns nothing. The Skill system prompt warned me explicitly: "NEVER skip hooks (--no-gpg-sign) unless user requests" — implying signing MAY be configured — but nothing in the repo requires it.

Verified via `git log --show-signature -5`: no SIGNED entries. Recent commits include both me (cc-pandas via `git -c user.name="cc-pandas"`) and the upstream Dicklesworthstone identity. Neither is cryptographically attested.

**Context — why this matters for a public library:**
- crates.io accepts publishes from anyone with API credentials + a matching Cargo.toml author. It doesn't verify commit signatures.
- docs.rs builds whatever source the tag points at. No signature check.
- The multi-agent swarm has several authorial identities mingling in `git log`. Auditing "did a real human approve this commit?" requires either (a) trusting `Author:` fields (which anyone can spoof) or (b) requiring signed commits.
- When the PyO3 binding ships (br-diic) + the release workflow lands (br-4clx), frankenpandas becomes a supply-chain dependency for downstream projects. Users of the Python wheel have no way to verify the tag that built it came from a trusted identity.
- Even within the swarm: if one compromised agent identity starts committing malicious code, reverting requires distinguishing legit commits from attack commits.

**Severity.** HIGH — supply-chain provenance for a library that will publish to crates.io. Low overhead to add; high cost to retrofit after a provenance incident.

**Fix shape:**

1. Documented policy in AGENTS.md + CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s):
   - All human contributors configure SSH commit signing (simpler than GPG, GitHub supports it since 2022):
       git config commit.gpgsign true
       git config gpg.format ssh
       git config user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
   - Swarm-agent identities publish their signing keys in a committed `AUTHORS.md` with public-key fingerprints. Identity-to-key mapping becomes auditable.
   - AGENTS.md gets a section: "How to prove your agent commits are yours, not spoofed."

2. CI gate:
   - ci.yml adds `- name: Verify commit signatures` running `git log --format='%G?' -1 <commit> | grep -q '^[GU]'` (G = good signature, U = good with unknown key).
   - Or: enforce via GitHub branch protection rule (toggle in repo settings) — requires signed commits to merge into main.

3. Release provenance:
   - br-4clx (release workflow) — tag signing: `git tag -s vX.Y.Z` with the release manager's key. crates.io publish reads the signed tag via the release-plz tooling; SLSA-level attestation becomes possible.
   - Consider Sigstore/cosign for supply-chain attestation alongside GPG signing — gives us a verifiable chain from source → tag → published crate.

4. Swarm agent identities:
   - Each agent that commits (cc-pandas, cod, etc.) has its own ssh signing key, committed public key in AUTHORS.md.
   - Commits tagged `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` get the Co-Authored-By line verified via the primary author's signature chain.

Blast radius: AGENTS.md addition (+~30 lines), AUTHORS.md (new, ~20 lines for current identities), ci.yml (+1 step ~10 lines), repo settings (branch protection toggle — requires repo admin). Zero source code changes.

Related:
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — policy docs live here.
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — private disclosure channel; the corresponding public provenance is addressed by this bead.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — tag signing integrated with the publish pipeline.
- br-36qc (cargo-audit) + br-hg60 (secret scan) — the same supply-chain hygiene family.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:41:17.728743310Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:41:17.728743310Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["provenance","review","security","supply-chain"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-d6xa","title":"[CRITICAL] conformance: 89 live_oracle_* tests silently skip in CI — core pandas-parity claim is not enforced","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Pattern 1 (Differential Testing) + the "Before claiming conformance, prove it" rule.

**Observation.** 89 live_oracle_*_matches_pandas tests exist in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs. Each one dispatches through capture_live_oracle_expected → subprocess pandas_oracle.py. The default HarnessConfig sets:

  oracle_root: repo_root.join("legacy_pandas_code/pandas")
  allow_system_pandas_fallback: false

When oracle_root.exists() is false, capture_live_oracle_expected returns HarnessError::OracleUnavailable. The 89 tests match on that variant and early-return with an eprintln!("live pandas unavailable; skipping ..."). No test fails. No test is marked ignored. No summary counter is emitted.

**The repo intentionally gitignores legacy_pandas_code/** (confirmed: .gitignore line "legacy_pandas_code/"). .github/workflows/ci.yml does NOT install pandas, does NOT set FP_PYTHON_BIN, does NOT checkout a pandas oracle source tree, does NOT set allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.

**Consequence.** In CI as shipped today, all 89 live_oracle_* tests silently pass by skipping. The entire differential conformance layer — the exact thing the README markets as "differential conformance tests verify against the actual pandas oracle" — is a no-op in CI. The only thing CI actually verifies is the 1249 pre-generated packet fixtures (FixtureExpected mode, golden-file comparison), which themselves were generated by some earlier pandas version that is not pinned anywhere (br-frankenpandas-8irq covers the version pin separately).

**Severity.** CRITICAL — the core product claim (pandas parity, verified by oracle) is not actually enforced by CI. Any regression that only the live oracle would catch (e.g. a new pandas point release changing semantics we match; a Rust-side bug that golden fixtures predate) lands on main silently.

**Fix shape:**
1. Count-and-fail: HarnessConfig gains a require_live_oracle: bool, default false. When true, OracleUnavailable converts to test failure instead of early-return. ci.yml sets the env var that flips this on.
2. CI installs pandas: ci.yml adds a Python setup step + pip install pandas==PINNED_VERSION (coordinates with br-frankenpandas-8irq). Then clones / mirrors legacy_pandas_code or points oracle_root at the pip-installed pandas via allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.
3. Skip counter: LiveOracleReport aggregates per-test skipped/run/failed counts, printed at end of lib-test run. CI asserts skipped_count == 0 or an explicit allowlist.
4. README update: add "When live pandas is unavailable, these 89 tests skip silently — not a gap in the oracle, a gap in the env" disclosure until (1)-(3) land.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+ ~12 lines), fp-conformance HarnessConfig + tests (+ ~60 lines for the counter + flag). No code-logic change.

Parallel bead: br-frankenpandas-8irq (pin pandas version) becomes a prerequisite for this bead — landing live-oracle-in-CI without a pinned pandas causes CI flakiness on any pandas update.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:10:40.629610903Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:23.420751862Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:23.418908373Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","critical","oracle","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-ddox","title":"[HIGH] docs: doctest never run in CI — 8 existing doctests silently unverified + docs.rs will fail at release","description":"Doctest coverage audit.

**Observation.**
- grep for `/// \`\`\`rust` / `/// \`\`\`` / `/// \`\`\`no_run` / `/// \`\`\`ignore` across crates/*/src/lib.rs finds only **8 doctests total** — 2 in frankenpandas, 4 in fp-index, 2 in fp-columnar. The 10 other crates have zero doctests.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `cargo test --workspace` at line 32 but does NOT run `cargo test --doc --workspace`. By default `cargo test --workspace` runs `#[test]` fns and integration tests but NOT doctests (those only run with `--doc` or when the default test target is configured to include them).
- Independent confirmation: I grepped for "doc" in ci.yml — returns only the cargo-llvm-cov-related word "Documentation" as false positive, no `--doc` invocation.

**Consequence.**
- The 8 existing doctests never execute in CI. A code example in a public README-level function that compiles today can be silently broken by an API change.
- More importantly, doctests are how Rust libraries document their API contracts with runnable examples. The public-API-contract bead (br-7cfm) called for documenting `# Panics:` on 1530 pub fns; good doctest coverage is the complementary half — runnable examples that VERIFY the documented behavior.
- docs.rs builds doctests when hosting crate docs. Once the release workflow lands (br-4clx) and docs.rs starts serving frankenpandas, any broken doctest fails the docs build → docs.rs shows "docs build failed" instead of the actual API reference → users land on a broken docs page from crates.io.

**Severity.** HIGH — compounds with br-7cfm (panic contract) and br-4clx (release). Without doctest coverage, the public API's "how do I use this?" examples rot silently until a docs.rs build fails publicly at release time.

**Fix shape:**

1. ci.yml adds `cargo test --doc --workspace` as a distinct step after the main `cargo test --workspace`. Catches doctest failures on every PR.
2. Add doctests to the existing pub fn docs systematically:
   - Priority 1: DataFrame constructors (from_dict, new, from_series, from_records) — most-used entry points.
   - Priority 2: Series arithmetic (add, sub, mul, div) — high visibility in README examples.
   - Priority 3: GroupBy core (sum, mean, agg_named, rolling) — conformance-heavy surfaces where doctest doubles as conformance sample.
   - Priority 4-N: Remaining public fns by frequency of use (informed by br-zk1j API matrix once that lands).

   Target: lift from 8 doctests to ~150 over several passes. Each passing doctest also serves as a user-facing example, reducing the pure `# Panics:` prose burden from br-7cfm.

3. `cargo test --doc --doc-args="..."`: for slow doctests, use `rust ignore` or `rust no_run` to bail out of execution while keeping the example visible on docs.rs.
4. Doctest regression: add a `scripts/doctest_count.sh` that fails CI if the doctest count shrinks between commits. Prevents silent removal of examples.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+1 step), ~150 lines of doctest additions (parallelizable across agents, one pub fn at a time), 1 small script. No source logic changes.

Related:
- br-7cfm (panic contract) — doctests verify the panic contract in addition to documenting it.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — doctests build on docs.rs at release; adding them now means no surprise at release time.
- br-zk1j (API matrix) — the matrix can mark "has doctest: yes/no" as an extra coverage column.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:57.255680319Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:57.255680319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","doctest","review"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-hg60","title":"[HIGH] security: no secret scanning in CI or pre-commit — leaked credentials in commits become permanent git history","description":"Secret / credential hygiene audit.

**Observation.**
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: no gitleaks, no truffleHog, no detect-secrets, no similar scanner.
- Repo has ONE pre-commit hook: `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` (coordination for the multi-agent swarm, not a secret scan).
- No `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (the pre-commit.com framework) — so no ecosystem-standard secret hooks either.
- No `.gitallowed` / `.gitleaksignore` / `.git-crypt` / `.gitattributes` with secret-relevant entries — nothing that suggests prior secret-scan discipline.

**Context.** This is a public-library repo that will ship to crates.io (once br-4clx lands). The multi-agent swarm produces 20+ commits/day from multiple authors. Real surface areas where secrets leak:
- CI artifacts: a test or fixture that inadvertently embeds a token (env var leaked into a string literal).
- Examples in rustdoc (doctests) pulling from real services with hardcoded API keys for demo purposes.
- Oracle script `oracle/pandas_oracle.py`: if it ever calls a remote service (it currently doesn't, but nothing prevents a PR adding one with a key).
- Sidecar config in `artifacts/`: JSON reports with paths, usernames, or host identifiers that constitute PII/infra info.

**Severity.** HIGH — git history is append-only for practical purposes. A secret committed today is discoverable by any scraper forever. Cleanup requires force-pushes (destructive, see skill rules about confirmations) plus rotation of every leaked credential.

**Fix shape:**

1. CI job `security`: runs gitleaks on every PR and on the `main` branch tip. Fails CI on any detection.
   ```yaml
   - uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
     env:
       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   ```
   Alternatively `trufflehog` for broader pattern coverage. Either is one step (~15s runtime).

2. Pre-commit hook `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/20-secret-scan.sh`:
   - Runs `gitleaks protect --staged` on every commit.
   - Exits non-zero on finding.
   - Extends the existing 50-agent-mail.py single-hook pattern. Use numeric prefix ordering so secret scan runs before agent-mail (fast-fail on credentials before coordinating the commit with other agents).

3. `.gitleaks.toml` config:
   - Allowlist obviously-non-secret patterns (fixture data, example API URLs, placeholder strings).
   - Tighten rulesets for AWS / GCP / GitHub / npm / Docker Hub / Slack webhook patterns.

4. Documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s): "Never commit real credentials. Use env vars for CI secrets via GitHub Actions secrets. All test fixtures must use placeholder tokens starting with `test_` or `fake_`."

5. One-time retroactive scan: run gitleaks against the entire repo history NOW (before the gate goes live) so any prior leaks can be rotated.

Blast radius: 1 new CI job, 1 new pre-commit hook, 1 new .gitleaks.toml, 1 section in CONTRIBUTING.md. Zero source code changes beyond any secrets that the retroactive scan surfaces.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — disclosure policy for external reports; this bead prevents us leaking secrets of our own.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — where the documented policy lives.
- br-36qc (cargo-audit) — detects vulns in deps; this bead detects secrets in our own commits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:40:36.501917176Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:40:36.501917176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pre-commit","review","secrets","security"]}

@@ -548,6 +551,7 @@

DataFrame.to_sql("results", con, index=True, index_label="row_id", if_exists="fail") -> None

+{"id":"frankenpandas-thty","title":"[HIGH] style: no rustfmt.toml — style opinions implicit + swarm agents burn cycles on format churn","description":"Style consistency audit.

**Observation.** No `rustfmt.toml` nor `.rustfmt.toml` exists at repo root or in any crate. CI runs `cargo fmt --check` (ci.yml line ~28) which enforces rustfmt's DEFAULT style — but the workspace has not captured any style-choice decisions.

**Consequence in an active multi-agent swarm.**
- Each agent's local rustfmt may differ from CI's if they use different nightly dates (see br-1d9y about nightly pin). A nightly-to-nightly rustfmt diff can reformat hundreds of lines silently, breaking `git diff` review for the PR.
- Opinions like `max_width = 100` (default) vs 120 (often preferred for wide dataframe column lists), `imports_granularity = "Crate"` vs "Module", `group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"`, `edition = "2024"` (important for the workspace's 2024-edition choice!) — all live in rustfmt.toml and all are currently unfixed, defaulting silently.
- Today's session: my edits to fp-frame/src/lib.rs (row_multiindex field, group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper) had my rustfmt reformat the surrounding `.groupby(&groupby_refs)` from one line to three (commit f7a4e59, original lib-updater noted the reformatting as incidental). With no rustfmt.toml, this is agent-specific churn, not a shared style decision.

**Secondary: `edition = "2024"` in per-crate Cargo.toml files is NOT sufficient for rustfmt** — rustfmt.toml has its own `edition = "2024"` field that the workspace currently doesn't set. Mismatch between Cargo.toml edition and rustfmt edition produces incorrect formatting in rare cases (2024-specific syntax like the `gen` keyword).

**Severity.** HIGH — compound cost across every PR. Each swarm agent burns cycles on style churn rather than code review. CI catches the final state but doesn't prevent the churn.

**Fix shape:**

`rustfmt.toml` at repo root, conservative but explicit:
```
edition = "2024"
max_width = 100
hard_tabs = false
tab_spaces = 4
newline_style = "Unix"
use_small_heuristics = "Default"
imports_granularity = "Crate"
group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"
reorder_imports = true
reorder_modules = true
unstable_features = true    # rustfmt grew many opinions behind nightly
```

(The project is already pinned to nightly via rust-toolchain.toml, so unstable rustfmt options are available.)

Also:
1. ci.yml's `cargo fmt --check` already works but gains enforcement of the explicit opinions once rustfmt.toml lands.
2. Pre-commit hook: add `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/10-rustfmt.sh` that runs `cargo fmt -- --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on staged .rs files) before commit. Fails fast locally, saves a CI round-trip.
3. Document the style choice in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling br-frankenpandas-6d5s).

Blast radius: 1 new file (~15 lines), 1 new pre-commit hook (~20 lines), possibly a one-time reformatting commit after rustfmt.toml lands (large diff but mechanical). Recommend batching the reformat into a single commit tagged `chore: apply rustfmt.toml opinions` that reviewers can fast-scroll past.

Related:
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — style notes live here.
- br-1d9y (nightly date pin) — rustfmt version implicitly pinned by the nightly pin, reducing reformat drift.
- br-iy8u (pre-commit doctrine) — adding a rustfmt hook extends the existing pre-commit discipline.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:17.551317919Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:17.551317919Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","rustfmt","style","swarm"]}
